steps.title: Identify what the context demands
steps.content: The sun is already below the horizon, yet it is manifest in "the livid tongues of fire darting towards the zenith." Something normally hidden becomes detectable through observable phenomena. The blank must mean able to be perceived or seen despite not being directly visible.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Situated: Describes a physical location or placement, not the quality of being detectable. A hidden sun being "situated" in fire tongues makes no sense. ✗
  • Choice B — Perceptible: Means visible or apparent to the senses — exactly what is happening as the buried sun reveals itself through the darting flames of light. ✓
  • Choice C — Realized: Implies achievement or fulfillment of a goal, not sensory visibility. The passage describes an observation, not an accomplishment. ✗
  • Choice D — Dwindling: Means decreasing or fading, which contradicts the role of the word here — the sun is not dwindling in the flames; it is becoming apparent through them. ✗
That is the correct answer.
